display.purpose: Brand mark and rare identity moments only.

body.purpose: Dense chat, settings, model picker, and navigation text.

label.purpose: Counts, status, keyboard hints, provider metadata, and small controls.

shadows.name: low-ambient
shadows.purpose: Light-mode raised controls and composer edge.

shadows.name: dark-ambient
shadows.purpose: Dark-mode overlays, popovers, and floating controls.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default duration range for hover, focus, open, close, and active-state transitions.

motion.name: overlay-enter
motion.purpose: Dialogs and dropdowns use short state motion from existing animate-in/animate-out utilities.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Signal Coral action button for new chat, send, save, and other primary commitments.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: var(--primary, #ff7770); color: var(--primary-foreground, #fff); font: 500 0.875rem/1.25 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs, 0 4px 10px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.03)); transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, border-color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary, #ff7770) 90%, #000); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #ff7770) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Quiet secondary control for toolbar, sidebar, sharing, and navigation actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #e7dbda); border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: var(--background, #fcf8f8); color: var(--foreground, #2e201f); font: 500 0.875rem/1.25 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs, 0 4px 10px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.03)); transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--accent, #fbdcda); color: var(--accent-foreground, #b81d14); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #ff7770) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Compact text input for search, settings, provider keys, and dense forms.
components.css: .ds-input-field { width: 100%; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem; border: 1px solid var(--input, #e7dbda); border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: transparent; color: var(--foreground, #2e201f); font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs, 0 4px 10px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.03)); transition: border-color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-input-field::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, #705e5c); opacity: 1; } .ds-input-field:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, #ff7770); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #ff7770) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Chat Composer
components.description: Floating command strip for model selection, attachments, image mode, prompt input, and send/stop.
components.css: .ds-chat-composer { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; max-width: 42rem; padding: 0.5rem; border: 2px solid var(--input, #e7dbda); border-radius: 0.6rem; background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background, #fcf8f8) 80%, transparent); color: var(--foreground, #2e201f); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs, 0 4px 10px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.03)); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); } .ds-composer-chip { border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--secondary, #ebe1e0) 70%, transparent); padding: 0.375rem 0.625rem; font: 400 0.75rem/1.2 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; } .ds-composer-input { flex: 1; color: var(--muted-foreground, #705e5c); font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; } .ds-composer-send { width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border: 1px solid var(--primary, #ff7770); border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: var(--primary, #ff7770); color: var(--primary-foreground, #fff); } .ds-composer-send:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #ff7770) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Sidebar Chat Item
components.description: Dense chat-history row with truncation, active state, and contextual actions.
components.css: .ds-sidebar-item { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 0.5rem; min-height: 2rem; padding: 0.375rem 0.5rem; border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); color: var(--sidebar-foreground, #5c403d); text-decoration: none; font: 400 0.875rem/1.3 Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out; } .ds-sidebar-item span { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-sidebar-item button { width: 1.5rem; height: 1.5rem; border: 0; border-radius: calc(0.6rem - 2px); background: transparent; color: inherit; opacity: 0.7; } .ds-sidebar-item:hover, .ds-sidebar-item-active { background: var(--sidebar-accent, #efe7e7); color: var(--foreground, #2e201f); } .ds-sidebar-item:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--ring, #ff7770) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Dialog Surface
components.description: Overlay panel for settings, auth-adjacent choices, and destructive confirmation flows.
components.html:
Settings

Provider API keys are stored only in your browser.
components.css: .ds-dialog-surface { width: min(100%, 34rem); padding: 1.5rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, #e7dbda); border-radius: 0.6rem; background: var(--background, #fcf8f8); color: var(--foreground, #2e201f); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg, 0 4px 10px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.05)); font-family: Inter, Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui; } .ds-dialog-surface h2 { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.35; font-weight: 600; } .ds-dialog-surface p { margin: 0; color: var(--muted-foreground, #705e5c); font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview:
Tezz Chat should feel like a compact command surface for high-context AI work: model choice, chat state, files, branching, search, and sharing are close to the user's hands. The system carries open-source confidence: polished enough to trust, inspectable enough to feel practical, and never dressed up in generic AI theater.

The current interface is a dense Nuxt/Vue chat app built from shadcn-vue primitives, Tailwind 4 theme variables, Pinia state, Reka UI overlays, and a warm Signal Coral accent. Its brand work is not decorative; it is the disciplined presentation of control. The product should be compact, precise, and legible under sustained use.

It explicitly rejects generic SaaS landing-page polish, toy chatbot behavior, enterprise dashboard sameness, and overbuilt devtool cosplay. The interface can be visually distinctive, but every visual decision must support speed, confidence, and state clarity.
keyCharacteristics: Dense AI-native workflow surface with minimal ceremony.
Warm rose-tinted neutral shell with Signal Coral reserved for primary action and active state.
Soft ambient lift for overlays, composer, popovers, and floating controls.
Inter/Geist UI typography with Syne reserved for the tezz wordmark.
Rose Pine syntax color only inside code and markdown output.

rules.name: The Signal Rarity Rule
rules.body: Signal Coral is for action, current selection, focus, and state. If more than one in ten visible elements is coral, the surface is shouting.

rules.name: The Syntax Containment Rule
rules.body: Rose Pine colors are for code intelligence and markdown output only. Do not leak syntax colors into general product chrome.

rules.name: The Wordmark-Only Display Rule
rules.body: Syne is a brand mark, not a UI font. Product surfaces should not become expressive at the cost of scan speed.

rules.name: The Mono Is Evidence Rule
rules.body: Monospace appears where the content is technical evidence: code, keyboard shortcuts, environment variables, and provider keys. Never use mono as a costume for "technical" brand flavor.

rules.name: The Ambient-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadows may clarify stacking and focus, but they must stay soft. If the shadow becomes a visible design object, it is too heavy.

rules.name: The Blur Must Earn It Rule
rules.body: Backdrop blur is allowed on the composer and modal overlay because it preserves context while prioritizing the active layer. Do not add glassmorphism panels for decoration.

dos: Do keep Signal Coral reserved for primary action, active state, focus, and provider/model selection.
Do preserve strong contrast for muted text; `--muted-foreground` is informational text, not decorative gray.
Do use Inter/Geist for dense UI and reserve Syne for the tezz wordmark or rare brand moments.
Do keep chat, model selection, BYOK setup, branching, sharing, and attachments visibly first-class.
Do use ambient lift for overlays, composer, dropdowns, and modals where depth clarifies interaction order.
Do keep keyboard access, focus rings, reduced-motion alternatives, and readable density as production requirements.
donts: Don't make tezz.chat feel like a generic SaaS landing page with vague gradients, interchangeable feature cards, hero metrics, or buzzword polish.
Don't make it feel like a toy chatbot with childish mascot energy, gimmicky bubbles, or unserious AI decoration.
Don't make it feel like an enterprise dashboard with sterile blue-gray sameness, heavy admin scaffolding, or corporate compliance aesthetics.
Don't make it feel like an overbuilt devtool through terminal cosplay, excessive monospace, or complexity that exists only to signal technical taste.
Don't add gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, repeated tiny uppercase eyebrows, or hero-metric blocks.
Don't add colored `border-left` or `border-right` stripes greater than 1px as card, list, callout, or active-row decoration; use full-row tint, full border, icon, or background state instead.
Don't use Rose Pine syntax colors outside code/markdown contexts.
